text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams manage changes efficiently. When developing a new feature, you create a separate **feature branch** from the main codebase. This allows you to work independently without affecting the stable version. Once your feature is complete, you can integrate it back through **merging**. Before merging, many teams use **pull requests** to review the code, discuss changes, and ensure quality. This creates a clear **version control workflow**: branches isolate development, pull requests enable collaboration, and merging unifies the work. By following this process, you maintain a clean history and reduce conflicts in the codebase.
